# orcasecurity_integration_servicenow_sir_schema (Data Source)

Reads the ServiceNow SIR field schema for a given credentials resource — the same
list the Orca UI shows when you build a SIR template. Useful when authoring or
auditing the `mapping_json` argument on
[`orcasecurity_integration_servicenow_sir_template`](../resources/integration_servicenow_sir_template.md).

Backs `GET /api/resources/{resource_id}/service_now/sir/schema`.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Read the list of fields a ServiceNow SIR resource exposes. Useful for authoring or
# auditing the `mapping_json` argument on `orcasecurity_integration_servicenow_sir_template`.
data "orcasecurity_integration_servicenow_sir_schema" "fields" {
  resource_id = "a6188626-fd15-4c0d-a262-9f3deffb5459"
}

output "sir_elements" {
  value = data.orcasecurity_integration_servicenow_sir_schema.fields.elements
}

output "sir_fields" {
  value = data.orcasecurity_integration_servicenow_sir_schema.fields.fields
}
```

## Argument Reference

* `resource_id` — (Required, String) ID of the SIR credentials resource (an
  `orcasecurity_integration_servicenow_resource` resource ID).

## Attribute Reference

* `elements` — (List of String) Flat list of field element names. These are the
  keys you can place in `mapping_json`.
* `fields` — (List of Object) Full schema entries. Each item exposes:
    * `element` — Field element name (the key used in `mapping_json`).
    * `label` — Human-friendly label as shown in ServiceNow.
    * `type` — Field type (`string`, `integer`, `reference`, etc.).
    * `default_value` — Default value Orca will use if you don't supply a mapping.
    * `max_length` — Max length the field accepts.
    * `choice` — Choice-list indicator if the field is a reference/picklist.
